content.text: Present your work clearly and do not leave any gaps in the proofs. Do all 9 problems.

problem.text: Suppose \(f\) is one-to-one and entire. Show that \(f(z)=az+b\), \(a\ne 0\).
Let \(J\ne 0\) be a complex number. Prove that if the series \(\sum_{n=0}^{\infty}a_nJ^n\) converges, then the power series \(\sum_{n=0}^{\infty}a_nz^n\) converges for \(|z|<|J|\).

subparts.label: a)
subparts.text: Let \(f(z)=\sum_{n=0}^{\infty}z^{2^n}\), \(|z|<1\). Show that the unit circle is a natural boundary for \(f\).

subparts.label: b)
subparts.text: Let \(g(z)=\prod_{n=0}^{\infty}(1+z^{2^n})\). Discuss the behavior of \(g(z)\) on \(|z|=1\).

problem.text: Find all entire functions \(f\) such that \(|f(z)|=1\) whenever \(|z|=1\).
Suppose \(f\) is analytic in the upper half-plane \(\operatorname{Im}z>0\) and \(|f|\le 1\). How large can \(|f'(i)|\) be? Find the extremal functions.

Suppose \(\{f_n\}\) is a sequence of analytic functions on the region \(D\), none of the functions \(f_n\) has a zero in \(D\), and \(\{f_n\}\) converges to \(f\) uniformly on compact subsets of \(D\). Prove that either \(f\) has no zero in \(D\) or \(f(z)=0\) for all \(z\) in \(D\).

subparts.label: a)
subparts.text: Suppose \(f\) is analytic in a region \(D\) containing the closed unit disc \(|z|\le 1\), \(|f(z)|>2\) if \(|z|=1\), and \(f(0)=1\). Must \(f\) have a zero in the unit disc?

subparts.label: b)
subparts.text: Suppose \(f\) and \(g\) are entire functions, and \(|f(z)|\le |g(z)|\) for every \(z\). What conclusion can you draw?

problem.text:
Suppose \(f\) is an entire function and \(f(0)=1\). Let
\[
M(r)=\sup_{0\le t\le 2\pi}|f(re^{it})|
\]
and \(n(r)\) be the number of zeros of \(f\) in the disc \(|z|\le r\). Prove that
\[
n(r)\ln 2\le \ln M(2r).
\]
